summary: Tested feed performance optimizations. Backend APIs (feed/explore/stories) all respond under 100ms. Fixed critical bug in stories endpoint (missing following_ids variable). Frontend uses SocialFeed.jsx instead of Feed.jsx - the optimizations in Feed.jsx (skeletons, lazy loading) are NOT being applied to the actual feed page.

critical.issue: Feed.jsx with optimizations (PostSkeleton, StorySkeleton, lazy loading) is NOT used. App routes /feed to SocialFeed.jsx instead
critical.details: App.js line 201 routes /feed to SocialFeed, not Feed. The optimizations in Feed.jsx are unused.

action_items: CRITICAL: Change App.js to use Feed.jsx instead of SocialFeed.jsx for /feed route, OR apply the optimizations (PostSkeleton, StorySkeleton, lazy loading images) to SocialFeed.jsx
The Feed.jsx file has all the optimizations but is not being used
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed bug in server.py line 867: stories endpoint was using undefined 'following_ids' variable - added missing line to extract following_ids from query result
Feed.jsx is imported in App.js but never used in any route
SocialFeed.jsx lacks loading skeletons and lazy loading optimizations that were added to Feed.jsx

success_rate.backend: 100% - All 9 tests passed, APIs respond under 100ms
success_rate.frontend: 80% - Feed loads but optimizations not applied (wrong component used)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend feed optimizations working. Frontend issue: App.js uses SocialFeed.jsx for /feed route, not Feed.jsx. The optimizations (skeletons, lazy loading) in Feed.jsx are not being applied.

stories_endpoint.issue: NameError: following_ids was used before being defined
stories_endpoint.fix: Added 'following_ids = [f["following_id"] for f in following]' before appending current user

features_verified.feed_page_shows_skeletons: FAIL - SocialFeed.jsx used instead of Feed.jsx
features_verified.refresh_feed_button: N/A - SocialFeed.jsx doesn't have refresh button
